visa_types.documents_required:
This list is intended to help you compile the documents for your visa application. Please note that the Embassy of Denmark (https://storbritannien.um.dk/en) may ask for additional documents or further information if this is needed to assess the application.
Please note that all copies and printouts attached to the application must be in color.
Original documents must be presented along with the presented color copies. 
All documents should also have a translation into English.
NB! Citizens of the Russian Federation must provide a biometric passport when applying for a type C visa and residence permit to Denmark. Non-biometric passports will not be accepted. Citizens of Belarus can provide documents with both a biometric and non-biometric passport.
Signed cover letter for an application form filled on https://applyvisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage). Signature must match the one in the passport. For minors: correctly signed application form by both parents. The application must be printed out in full in color (including the visa fee payment confirmation page).
- Foreign passport valid for at least three months after the intended date of (last) departure from the Schengen area, containing at least two blank pages and issued within the
                                  previous ten years.
                                  Additionally:
- If available other valid or/and expired foreign passports
- Two recent color photographs (3,5 x 4,5 cm) on the light background. Image has to bear a true likeness to the applicant. Photographs have to conform to the requirements of International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO). (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5Yzcxeg1mIivv7K7iVCq1v/78dca573e8e0a32b5254f31cdc701077/photograph-guidelines_5f0a8bf4945bbd14eb30b9c903750e4311cf71aa5d53dd822ee3ee23d9f576e0.pdf)
Due to the stringent requirements around the Photo required for your application, we recommend you utilize the photo services available inside the Visa Application Center. This
                                  will provide you assurance that your application is submitted with the photograph that meets the specifications and will be acceptable to the Embassy. You will have ready
                                  photographs in 45 seconds. 
                                  Fees: 
                                  The cost of the service is 360 RUB for 6 photographs.
                                  How to book: 
                                  This service can be booked at the visa application centre
                                  upon your arrival.
- Visa fee in line with the categories of the visa facilitation agreements with specific countries and the Visa Code.
- Travel medical insurance covering the entire period of intended stay, valid for all Schengen States and covering any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for
                                medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be €30.000.
- Proof of funds with the history of 3 months, even if expenses are covered by the host.
- Transport documents (flight or other booking).
- Proof of accommodation.
- Color copy of the internal passport (issued at the age of 14): all pages
Please, pay attention, you will need to provide your internal passport in original as well
- In cases of minors:
- Original and a Color copy of birth certificate.
- Consent of the parental authority or legal guardian should be required only if the minor travels alone or only with one parent. Exceptions should be made to this if the single
                                    parent with whom the minor is to travel holds the parental authority alone (i.e. in cases where the other parent has deceased or been deprived of custody, e.g. a death
                                    certificate of the other parent or a court degree vesting custody exclusively in the parent who is signing the application would be requested).
- Color copy of the valid Schengen visa of the parent(s) travelling with the minor for whom a visa is not applied at the same time with the parent(s).
- In cases of third-country nationals:
- Proof of legal residence in the Belarus, valid for at least three months after the planned return from the Schengen area or a document proving that the applicant has applied for a
                                renewal of proof of legal residence.
- Proof of employment:
For employees – verification from the applicant’s employer with the company’s details, stamp and signature of an executive officer. It should include the following information:
date of employment;
position; information about salary.
For self-employed people – Proof of economic activity: an original (and a copy) of the certificate of registration of an individual entrepreneur, an original (and a copy of the certificate of registration in the Tax Inspectorate, a copy of income statement for the accounting period stamped by Tax Inspectorate).
If the application is not submitted personally by the applicant, the applicant must issue a notarized Power of Attorney to the person handing in the application. Also if the passport is collected by a third party, a notarized Power of Attorney is required.
For close relatives the document proving family relationship is sufficient. Accredited travel agencies and Red Carpet companies are exempted from this requirement.

- Participants in scientific, cultural and artistic activities
- Invitation letter or written request from the host organization (competent authority, national sport federation or national Olympic committee).
The following shall be indicated:
- information about the host organization;
- Information about the invited person;
- information on the role of the person;
- Purpose of travel;
- Dates of visit (period, number of days);
- Information about financial conditions of the visit (who is responsible for the costs).
- Confirmation of the sport qualification in case not already sufficiently demonstrated in the invitation or written request
- If the host party is not financially responsible for all expenses regarding the trip, it is necessary to provide one of the following documentation: 
-  statement on sponsorship/letter of financial guarantee from the sending party
or 
- bank/credit card statement(s) confirming that the applicant has 70 EUROS per day (50 EUROS per day if lodging in a hostel or other budget accommodation)
or 
- other proof of financial means.
- Participants in international sports events and accompanying persons in their professional capacity 
- Invitation letter or written request from the host organization (competent authority, national sport federation or national Olympic committee), including information
                                      on the role of the persons.
The following shall be indicated:
- information about the host organization;
- information about the invited person;
- information on the role of the person;
- purpose of travel;
- dates of visit (period, number of days);
- information about financial conditions of the visit (who is responsible for the costs).
- If the host party is not financially responsible for all expenses regarding the trip, it is necessary to provide one of the following documentation:
- statement on sponsorship/letter of financial guarantee from the sending party
                                          or 
- bank/credit card statement(s) confirming that the applicant has 70 EUROS per day (50 EUROS per day if lodging in a hostel or other budget accommodation)
                                          or 
- other proof of financial means.
- Pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers
- Invitation letter or written request, or certificate of enrolment or student card by the host university, academy, institute, college or school, or certificate of
                                      the courses to be attended. 
The following shall be indicated:
- Information about the host party;
- Information about the applicant;
- Purpose of travel;
- Dates of visit (period, number of days);
- Information about financial conditions of the visit (who is responsible for the costs);
- Confirmation of the sport qualification in case not already sufficiently demonstrated in the invitation or written request
- If the host party is not financially responsible for all expenses regarding the trip, it is necessary to provide one of the following documentation:
- statement on sponsorship/letter of financial guarantee from the sending party
                                          or 
-  bank/credit card statement(s) confirming that the applicant has 70 EUROS per day (50 EUROS per day if lodging in a hostel or other budget accommodation)
                                          or 
- other proof of financial means.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.

visa_types.processing_time:
Average processing time for visa applications is 15 days, when all required documentation has been provided. This period does not include the time of delivery of documents from Minsk to London and back. Delivery may take up to 27 business days.
The case processing time is calculated from the day the Embassy register the application in the visa system. It is not calculated from the time you apply online or submit the application at a VFS centre. 
Please click here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service?anchor=09F4571341A14D82A430ACFF3113733A) for processing time information for Residence and Work Permits.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Submission of documents is carried out by preliminary registration through our website (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/blr/en/dnk/book-an-appointment). If you are planning to apply for a type C visa (Schengen visa), please select the “Short term - C visa” category when registering.
If you wish to visit Denmark for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Denmark.
Please visit Danish Immigration (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Short-stay-visa/Business-visits) for more details.
The Schengen calculator (http://ec.europa.eu/dgs/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/border-crossing/schengen_calculator_en.html) can be used for calculating the travel days remaining under a Schengen short-stay visa according to the 90-days/180-days rule.
The user's guide (https://ec.europa.eu/assets/home/visa-calculator/docs/short_stay_schengen_calculator_user_manual_en.pdf) contains information on the applicable rules, the use of the calculator and practical examples.

## Overview
Submission of documents is carried out by preliminary registration through our website (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/blr/en/dnk/book-an-appointment). If you are planning to apply for a type C visa (Schengen visa), please select the “Short term - C visa” category when registering.
If you wish to visit Denmark for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Denmark.
Please visit Danish Immigration (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Short-stay-visa/Business-visits) for more details.
The Schengen calculator (http://ec.europa.eu/dgs/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/border-crossing/schengen_calculator_en.html) can be used for calculating the travel days remaining under a Schengen short-stay visa according to the 90-days/180-days rule.
The user's guide (https://ec.europa.eu/assets/home/visa-calculator/docs/short_stay_schengen_calculator_user_manual_en.pdf) contains information on the applicable rules, the use of the calculator and practical examples.

All documents should also have a translation into English.

For minors: correctly signed application form by both parents. The application must be printed out in full in color (including the visa fee payment confirmation page).
- Foreign passport valid for at least three months after the intended date of (last) departure from the Schengen area, containing at least two blank pages and issued within the
                                  previous ten years.
                                  Additionally:
- If available other valid or/and expired foreign passports
- Two recent color photographs (3,5 x 4,5 cm) on the light background. Image has to bear a true likeness to the applicant. Photographs have to conform to the requirements of International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO). (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5Yzcxeg1mIivv7K7iVCq1v/78dca573e8e0a32b5254f31cdc701077/photograph-guidelines_5f0a8bf4945bbd14eb30b9c903750e4311cf71aa5d53dd822ee3ee23d9f576e0.pdf)

                                  will provide you assurance that your application is submitted with the photograph that meets the specifications and will be acceptable to the Embassy. You will have ready
                                  photographs in 45 seconds. 
                                  Fees: 
                                  The cost of the service is 360 RUB for 6 photographs.
                                  How to book: 
                                  This service can be booked at the visa application centre
                                  upon your arrival.
- Visa fee in line with the categories of the visa facilitation agreements with specific countries and the Visa Code.
- Travel medical insurance covering the entire period of intended stay, valid for all Schengen States and covering any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for
                                medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be €30.000.
- Proof of funds with the history of 3 months, even if expenses are covered by the host.
- Transport documents (flight or other booking).
- Proof of accommodation.
- Color copy of the internal passport (issued at the age of 14): all pages
Please, pay attention, you will need to provide your internal passport in original as well
- In cases of minors:
- Original and a Color copy of birth certificate.
- Consent of the parental authority or legal guardian should be required only if the minor travels alone or only with one parent. Exceptions should be made to this if the single

- Color copy of the valid Schengen visa of the parent(s) travelling with the minor for whom a visa is not applied at the same time with the parent(s).
- In cases of third-country nationals:
- Proof of legal residence in the Belarus, valid for at least three months after the planned return from the Schengen area or a document proving that the applicant has applied for a
                                renewal of proof of legal residence.
- Proof of employment:
For employees – verification from the applicant’s employer with the company’s details, stamp and signature of an executive officer. It should include the following information:
date of employment;
position; information about salary.
For self-employed people – Proof of economic activity: an original (and a copy) of the certificate of registration of an individual entrepreneur, an original (and a copy of the certificate of registration in the Tax Inspectorate, a copy of income statement for the accounting period stamped by Tax Inspectorate).

For close relatives the document proving family relationship is sufficient. Accredited travel agencies and Red Carpet companies are exempted from this requirement.

## DOCUMENTS REQUIRED AS PER CATEGORY OF TRAVELLER:
- Participants in scientific, cultural and artistic activities
- Invitation letter or written request from the host organization (competent authority, national sport federation or national Olympic committee).
The following shall be indicated:
- information about the host organization;
- Information about the invited person;
- information on the role of the person;
- Purpose of travel;
- Dates of visit (period, number of days);
- Information about financial conditions of the visit (who is responsible for the costs).
- Confirmation of the sport qualification in case not already sufficiently demonstrated in the invitation or written request
- If the host party is not financially responsible for all expenses regarding the trip, it is necessary to provide one of the following documentation: 
-  statement on sponsorship/letter of financial guarantee from the sending party
or 
- bank/credit card statement(s) confirming that the applicant has 70 EUROS per day (50 EUROS per day if lodging in a hostel or other budget accommodation)
or 
- other proof of financial means.
- Participants in international sports events and accompanying persons in their professional capacity 
- Invitation letter or written request from the host organization (competent authority, national sport federation or national Olympic committee), including information
                                      on the role of the persons.
The following shall be indicated:
- information about the host organization;
- information about the invited person;
- information on the role of the person;
- purpose of travel;
- dates of visit (period, number of days);
- information about financial conditions of the visit (who is responsible for the costs).
- If the host party is not financially responsible for all expenses regarding the trip, it is necessary to provide one of the following documentation:
- statement on sponsorship/letter of financial guarantee from the sending party
                                          or 
- bank/credit card statement(s) confirming that the applicant has 70 EUROS per day (50 EUROS per day if lodging in a hostel or other budget accommodation)
                                          or 
- other proof of financial means.
- Pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers
- Invitation letter or written request, or certificate of enrolment or student card by the host university, academy, institute, college or school, or certificate of
                                      the courses to be attended. 
The following shall be indicated:
- Information about the host party;
- Information about the applicant;
- Purpose of travel;
- Dates of visit (period, number of days);
- Information about financial conditions of the visit (who is responsible for the costs);
- Confirmation of the sport qualification in case not already sufficiently demonstrated in the invitation or written request
- If the host party is not financially responsible for all expenses regarding the trip, it is necessary to provide one of the following documentation:
- statement on sponsorship/letter of financial guarantee from the sending party
                                          or 
-  bank/credit card statement(s) confirming that the applicant has 70 EUROS per day (50 EUROS per day if lodging in a hostel or other budget accommodation)
                                          or 
- other proof of financial means.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.

## Photo Specifications
Size of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the
                              head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above
                              format. 
Applicants may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
Average processing time for visa applications is 15 days, when all required documentation has been provided. This period does not include the time of delivery of documents from Minsk to London and back. Delivery may take up to 27 business days.
The case processing time is calculated from the day the Embassy register the application in the visa system. It is not calculated from the time you apply online or submit the application at a VFS centre. 
Please click here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service?anchor=09F4571341A14D82A430ACFF3113733A) for processing time information for Residence and Work Permits.

visa_types.documents_required:
This list is intended to help you compile the documents for your visa application. Please note that the Embassy of Denmark (https://storbritannien.um.dk/en) may ask for additional documents or further information if this is needed to assess the application.
Citizens of the Russian Federation must provide a biometric passport when applying for a type C visa. Non-biometric passports will not be accepted. 
Citizens of Belarus can provide documents with both a biometric and non-biometric passport.
Please note that all copies attached to the application must be color copies.
Original documents must be presented along with the presented color copies. If you provide certified documents that are stapled together, you must additionally attach a color copy of them.
All documents should also have a translation into English. The translation may not be certified.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
GENERAL LIST OF REQUIRED DOCUMENTS
- Signed cover letter for an application form
filled on
https://applyvisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk)
. Signature must match the one in the passport. For minors: Cover letter must be signed by one of their parents or a legal guardian. The application must be printed out in full in color (including the visa fee payment confirmation page).
- Signed current foreign passport (+ color copy of all pages)
– issued within the last 10 years, have 2 blank pages and be valid for at least 3 months beyond your intended stay in the Schengen area. Color copies of passport pages with personal data must be provided in duplicate.
Additionally:
- If available, previous foreign passports – color copy of the all previous Schengen visas and stamps must be provided.
- For citizens of Belarus with a biometric passport: color copy of the ID card.
- For citizens of the Russian Federation: color copy of the internal passport (issued at the age of 14): all filled in pages.
- Two recent color photographs
(3,5 x 4,5 cm) on the light background. Image has to bear a true likeness to the applicant. Photographs have to conform to the
requirements of International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/denmark/Belarus/english/pdf/photograph-guidelines_5f0a8bf4945bbd14eb30b9c903750e4311cf71aa5d53dd822ee3ee23d9f576e0.pdf)
.
- Visa fee
in line with the categories of the visa facilitation agreements with specific countries and the Visa Code.
- Travel medical insurance
covering the entire period of intended stay, valid for all Schengen States and covering any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be €30.000.
- Proof of sufficient funds for the stay
with the history of 3 months – approx. £60 per day.
- Invitation from Danish host
– online invitation ID number (VU1), signed and dated invitation form (VU5) or signed and dated invitation on letterhead and with Danish CVR-number. Must explain purpose and dates of visit, as well as include contact details of the host. The Embassy of Denmark in London recommends that your host use the relevant invitation form from
newtodenmark.dk (https://nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Short-stay-visa/Invitation-of-a-visa-applicant)
.
- Proof of travel plan
– e.g. reserved return ticket and reserved accommodation or sufficient means to cover such costs. The Embassy of Denmark in London recommends that you do not buy any tickets before your visa has been approved.
- Evidence of occupation
– on letterhead, signed, and less than 1 month old.
- Employment: Letter from employer incl. salary and position of applicant and 3 months’ payslips
- Self-employed: accountant letter incl. annual salary drawn and self-assessment form edited by revenue and customs authorities.
- In cases of third-country nationals:
- Proof of legal residence in the Belarus, valid for at least three months after the planned return from the Schengen area

- Members of official delegations 
- Letter (e.g. verbal note) issued by a competent Belarussian authority (e.g. MID) confirming that the applicant is a member of its delegation travelling to the Schengen Member
                                    State.
- Color copy of the official invitation.
- Employees travelling on business 
- Invitation letter or written request from a hosting company, organisation, authority or organising committee of trade and industrial exhibitions to attend meetings or
                                      events connected with trade, industry or work.
The following shall be indicated:
- Information about the host party;
- Information about the applicant;
- Purpose of travel;
- Dates of visit (period, number of days);
- Information about financial conditions of the visit (who is responsible for the costs);
- If no such invitation or written request can be obtained, other proof of purpose of stay (e.g. information on participation in the event, entrance ticket to trade fair,
                                      business correspondence, programme of the business trip).
- In case not already sufficiently demonstrated in the invitation or written request, proof of employment e.g. verification from the applicant’s employer with the company’s
                                      details, stamp and signature of an executive stuff. 
The following shall be indicated:
- date of employment;
- position.
- If the host party is not financially responsible for all expenses regarding the trip, it is necessary to provide one of the following documentation:
- statement on sponsorship/letter of financial guarantee from the sending party or
- bank/credit card statement(s) confirming that the applicant has 70 EUROS per day (50 EUROS per day if lodging in a hostel or other budget accommodation) or
- other proof of financial means.
- Self-employed people travelling on business 
- Invitation letter or written request from a hosting company, organization, authority or organizing committee of trade and industrial exhibitions to attend meetings or
                                      events connected with trade, industry or work.
The following shall be indicated:
- Information about the host party;
- Information about the applicant;
- Purpose of travel;
- Information about financial conditions of the visit (who is responsible for the costs);
- If no such invitation or written request can be obtained, other proof of purpose of stay (e.g. information on participation in the event, entrance ticket to trade fair, business
                                    correspondence, programme of the business trip).
- Proof of economic activity: an original (and a copy) of the certificate of registration of an individual entrepreneur, an original (and a copy of the certificate of registration
                                    in the Tax Inspectorate, a copy of income statement for the accounting period stamped by Tax Inspectorate).
- If the host party is not financially responsible for all expenses regarding the trip, it is necessary to provide one of the following documentation:
- statement on sponsorship/letter of financial guarantee or
- bank/credit card statement(s) confirming that the applicant has 70 EUROS per day (50 EUROS per day if lodging in a hostel or other budget accommodation) or
- other proof of financial means
- Participation in a Conference or Congress 
- Invitation letter or written request from a hosting company, organization, authority, or organizing committee of a Conference or Congress. 
The following shall be indicated:
- Information about the host party;
- Information about the applicant;
- Purpose of travel;
- Dates of visit (period, number of days);
- Information about financial conditions of the visit (who is responsible for the costs);
If no such invitation or written request can be obtained, other proof of purpose of visit e.g.:
- confirmation of payment of the registration fee for participation in a Conference or Congress,
- business correspondence,
- programme of the event.
- Proof of employment, in case not already sufficiently demonstrated in the invitation or written request. Proof of employment:
- For employees – verification from the applicant’s employer with the company’s details, stamp and signature of an executive officer. It should include the following information:
- date of employment;
- position;
- For self-employed people – Proof of economic activity: an original (and a copy) of the certificate of registration of an individual entrepreneur, an original (and a copy
                                      of the certificate of registration in the Tax Inspectorate, a copy of income statement for the accounting period stamped by Tax Inspectorate).
- For students and scholars (over 14 years of age) – verification from university or school.
- If the host party is not financially responsible for all expenses regarding the trip, it is necessary to provide one of the following documentation:
- statement on sponsorship/letter of financial guarantee (e.g. from the sending party) or
- bank/credit card statement(s) confirming that the applicant has 70 EUROS per day (50 EUROS per day if lodging in a hostel or other budget accommodation) or
- other proof of financial means
- Drivers (international cargo and passengers)
- Invitation letter from Denmark or written request from the Belarussian national association of carriers (BAMAP), or the national association of carriers from Denmark.
The following shall be indicated:
- Information about the inviting or sending company or organization;
- Information about the applicant;
- Information about purpose, duration and frequency of trip(s);
- Dates of visit (period, number of days);
- Information about financial conditions of the visit (who is responsible for the costs).
- Color copy (verified by stamp of the employer) of the contract of employment or work-book.
- International carriers license of the Belarusian (Russian) company (colour copy verified by stamp of the employer).
- Original of previous attachment to truck driver’s visa, if available.
- Seamen (members of ship crew) 
- Color copy of a seafarer's identity document.
- Color copy of a contract of employment.
- Invitation letter from shipping company
- Information about the inviting company or organization;
- Information about the invited person;
- Information about purpose, duration and frequency of trip(s);
- Dates of visit (period, number of days);
- Information about financial conditions of the visit (who is responsible for the costs).
- Journalists
- A certificate or other document issued by a professional organization proving that the person is a qualified journalist and a color copy.
- Letter from his/her employer stating that the purpose of the journey is to carry out journalistic work.
- For freelance journalists:
- proof of journalistic activity (e.g. a press card or work contract) and
- proof of economic activity (e.g. 2NDFL or 3NDFL form, or excerpt from the trade/fiscal registry, or certificate for individual business, or bank statement(s) covering
                                        at least three preceding months).
- Information about financial conditions of the visit (e.g. statement on sponsorship/letter of financial guarantee from the sending party, bank/credit card statement(s)
                                      confirming that the applicant has 70 EUROS per day (50 EUROS per day if lodging in a hostel or other budget accommodation), or other proof of financial means).
- Other short-term (90/180 days) labourers (if permissible with Schengen visa and relevant national law)
- Letter from the employer and/or employment contract.
- Information about financial conditions of the visit.
- Members of train, refrigerator and locomotive crews 
- Written request from the competent Belarussian railway company or a competent railway company of Denmark stating the purpose, duration and frequency of trips.
- Proof of employment or copy of the work-book.
- Information about financial conditions of the visit.
- Participants in official twin city programs 
- Written request (letter) from the head of administration/mayor of the city where the twinning activity is to take place.
- Color copy of the official invitation from the head of administration/mayor of the sending city with full details of the participant.
- Air crews (if in need of a visa) 
- Letter from the operating body.

Overview
Submission of documents is carried out by preliminary registration through our website (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/blr/en/dnk/book-an-appointment). If you are planning to apply for a type C visa (Schengen visa), please select the “Short term - C visa” category when registering.
If you wish to visit Denmark for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Denmark.
Please visit Danish Immigration (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Short-stay-visa/Business-visits) for more details.
The Schengen calculator (http://ec.europa.eu/dgs/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/border-crossing/schengen_calculator_en.html) can be used for calculating the travel days remaining under a Schengen short-stay visa according to the 90-days/180-days rule.
The user's guide (https://ec.europa.eu/assets/home/visa-calculator/docs/short_stay_schengen_calculator_user_manual_en.pdf) contains information on the applicable rules, the use of the calculator and practical examples.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Submission of documents is carried out by preliminary registration through our website (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/blr/en/dnk/book-an-appointment). If you are planning to apply for a type C visa (Schengen visa), please select the “Short term - C visa” category when registering.
If you wish to visit Denmark for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Denmark.
Please visit Danish Immigration (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Short-stay-visa/Business-visits) for more details.
The Schengen calculator (http://ec.europa.eu/dgs/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/border-crossing/schengen_calculator_en.html) can be used for calculating the travel days remaining under a Schengen short-stay visa according to the 90-days/180-days rule.
The user's guide (https://ec.europa.eu/assets/home/visa-calculator/docs/short_stay_schengen_calculator_user_manual_en.pdf) contains information on the applicable rules, the use of the calculator and practical examples.

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Business]
*This fee covers citizens of Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, the Former Yugoslav Republic of Macedonia, Moldova, Montenegro, Serbia and Ukraine
Please click here to check visa fee (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
Please Note:
- The service charge is 76 €.The service charge will be collected in Belarusian Rubles equivalent as per the exchange rate of National Bank of Belarus (inclusive of VAT).The above fees
                                is levied per application over and above the visa fees
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from Additional Services tab.

## Documents Required
This list is intended to help you compile the documents for your visa application. Please note that the Embassy of Denmark (https://storbritannien.um.dk/en) may ask for additional documents or further information if this is needed to assess the application.
Citizens of the Russian Federation must provide a biometric passport when applying for a type C visa. Non-biometric passports will not be accepted. 
Citizens of Belarus can provide documents with both a biometric and non-biometric passport.
Please note that all copies attached to the application must be color copies.
Original documents must be presented along with the presented color copies. If you provide certified documents that are stapled together, you must additionally attach a color copy of them.
All documents should also have a translation into English. The translation may not be certified.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
GENERAL LIST OF REQUIRED DOCUMENTS
- Signed cover letter for an application form
filled on
https://applyvisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk)
. Signature must match the one in the passport. For minors: Cover letter must be signed by one of their parents or a legal guardian. The application must be printed out in full in color (including the visa fee payment confirmation page).
- Signed current foreign passport (+ color copy of all pages)
– issued within the last 10 years, have 2 blank pages and be valid for at least 3 months beyond your intended stay in the Schengen area. Color copies of passport pages with personal data must be provided in duplicate.
Additionally:
- If available, previous foreign passports – color copy of the all previous Schengen visas and stamps must be provided.
- For citizens of Belarus with a biometric passport: color copy of the ID card.
- For citizens of the Russian Federation: color copy of the internal passport (issued at the age of 14): all filled in pages.
- Two recent color photographs
(3,5 x 4,5 cm) on the light background. Image has to bear a true likeness to the applicant. Photographs have to conform to the
requirements of International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/denmark/Belarus/english/pdf/photograph-guidelines_5f0a8bf4945bbd14eb30b9c903750e4311cf71aa5d53dd822ee3ee23d9f576e0.pdf)
.
- Visa fee
in line with the categories of the visa facilitation agreements with specific countries and the Visa Code.
- Travel medical insurance
covering the entire period of intended stay, valid for all Schengen States and covering any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be €30.000.
- Proof of sufficient funds for the stay
with the history of 3 months – approx. £60 per day.
- Invitation from Danish host
– online invitation ID number (VU1), signed and dated invitation form (VU5) or signed and dated invitation on letterhead and with Danish CVR-number. Must explain purpose and dates of visit, as well as include contact details of the host. The Embassy of Denmark in London recommends that your host use the relevant invitation form from
newtodenmark.dk (https://nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Short-stay-visa/Invitation-of-a-visa-applicant)
.
- Proof of travel plan
– e.g. reserved return ticket and reserved accommodation or sufficient means to cover such costs. The Embassy of Denmark in London recommends that you do not buy any tickets before your visa has been approved.
- Evidence of occupation
– on letterhead, signed, and less than 1 month old.
- Employment: Letter from employer incl. salary and position of applicant and 3 months’ payslips
- Self-employed: accountant letter incl. annual salary drawn and self-assessment form edited by revenue and customs authorities.
- In cases of third-country nationals:
- Proof of legal residence in the Belarus, valid for at least three months after the planned return from the Schengen area

## DOCUMENTS REQUIRED AS PER CATEGORY OF TRAVELLER:
- Members of official delegations 
- Letter (e.g. verbal note) issued by a competent Belarussian authority (e.g. MID) confirming that the applicant is a member of its delegation travelling to the Schengen Member
                                    State.
- Color copy of the official invitation.
- Employees travelling on business 
- Invitation letter or written request from a hosting company, organisation, authority or organising committee of trade and industrial exhibitions to attend meetings or
                                      events connected with trade, industry or work.
The following shall be indicated:
- Information about the host party;
- Information about the applicant;
- Purpose of travel;
- Dates of visit (period, number of days);
- Information about financial conditions of the visit (who is responsible for the costs);
- If no such invitation or written request can be obtained, other proof of purpose of stay (e.g. information on participation in the event, entrance ticket to trade fair,
                                      business correspondence, programme of the business trip).
- In case not already sufficiently demonstrated in the invitation or written request, proof of employment e.g. verification from the applicant’s employer with the company’s
                                      details, stamp and signature of an executive stuff. 
The following shall be indicated:
- date of employment;
- position.
- If the host party is not financially responsible for all expenses regarding the trip, it is necessary to provide one of the following documentation:
- statement on sponsorship/letter of financial guarantee from the sending party or
- bank/credit card statement(s) confirming that the applicant has 70 EUROS per day (50 EUROS per day if lodging in a hostel or other budget accommodation) or
- other proof of financial means.
- Self-employed people travelling on business 
- Invitation letter or written request from a hosting company, organization, authority or organizing committee of trade and industrial exhibitions to attend meetings or
                                      events connected with trade, industry or work.
The following shall be indicated:
- Information about the host party;
- Information about the applicant;
- Purpose of travel;
- Information about financial conditions of the visit (who is responsible for the costs);
- If no such invitation or written request can be obtained, other proof of purpose of stay (e.g. information on participation in the event, entrance ticket to trade fair, business
                                    correspondence, programme of the business trip).
- Proof of economic activity: an original (and a copy) of the certificate of registration of an individual entrepreneur, an original (and a copy of the certificate of registration
                                    in the Tax Inspectorate, a copy of income statement for the accounting period stamped by Tax Inspectorate).
- If the host party is not financially responsible for all expenses regarding the trip, it is necessary to provide one of the following documentation:
- statement on sponsorship/letter of financial guarantee or
- bank/credit card statement(s) confirming that the applicant has 70 EUROS per day (50 EUROS per day if lodging in a hostel or other budget accommodation) or
- other proof of financial means
- Participation in a Conference or Congress 
- Invitation letter or written request from a hosting company, organization, authority, or organizing committee of a Conference or Congress. 
The following shall be indicated:
- Information about the host party;
- Information about the applicant;
- Purpose of travel;
- Dates of visit (period, number of days);
- Information about financial conditions of the visit (who is responsible for the costs);
If no such invitation or written request can be obtained, other proof of purpose of visit e.g.:
- confirmation of payment of the registration fee for participation in a Conference or Congress,
- business correspondence,
- programme of the event.
- Proof of employment, in case not already sufficiently demonstrated in the invitation or written request. Proof of employment:
- For employees – verification from the applicant’s employer with the company’s details, stamp and signature of an executive officer. It should include the following information:
- date of employment;
- position;
- For self-employed people – Proof of economic activity: an original (and a copy) of the certificate of registration of an individual entrepreneur, an original (and a copy
                                      of the certificate of registration in the Tax Inspectorate, a copy of income statement for the accounting period stamped by Tax Inspectorate).
- For students and scholars (over 14 years of age) – verification from university or school.
- If the host party is not financially responsible for all expenses regarding the trip, it is necessary to provide one of the following documentation:
- statement on sponsorship/letter of financial guarantee (e.g. from the sending party) or
- bank/credit card statement(s) confirming that the applicant has 70 EUROS per day (50 EUROS per day if lodging in a hostel or other budget accommodation) or
- other proof of financial means
- Drivers (international cargo and passengers)
- Invitation letter from Denmark or written request from the Belarussian national association of carriers (BAMAP), or the national association of carriers from Denmark.
The following shall be indicated:
- Information about the inviting or sending company or organization;
- Information about the applicant;
- Information about purpose, duration and frequency of trip(s);
- Dates of visit (period, number of days);
- Information about financial conditions of the visit (who is responsible for the costs).
- Color copy (verified by stamp of the employer) of the contract of employment or work-book.
- International carriers license of the Belarusian (Russian) company (colour copy verified by stamp of the employer).
- Original of previous attachment to truck driver’s visa, if available.
- Seamen (members of ship crew) 
- Color copy of a seafarer's identity document.
- Color copy of a contract of employment.
- Invitation letter from shipping company
- Information about the inviting company or organization;
- Information about the invited person;
- Information about purpose, duration and frequency of trip(s);
- Dates of visit (period, number of days);
- Information about financial conditions of the visit (who is responsible for the costs).
- Journalists
- A certificate or other document issued by a professional organization proving that the person is a qualified journalist and a color copy.
- Letter from his/her employer stating that the purpose of the journey is to carry out journalistic work.
- For freelance journalists:
- proof of journalistic activity (e.g. a press card or work contract) and
- proof of economic activity (e.g. 2NDFL or 3NDFL form, or excerpt from the trade/fiscal registry, or certificate for individual business, or bank statement(s) covering
                                        at least three preceding months).
- Information about financial conditions of the visit (e.g. statement on sponsorship/letter of financial guarantee from the sending party, bank/credit card statement(s)
                                      confirming that the applicant has 70 EUROS per day (50 EUROS per day if lodging in a hostel or other budget accommodation), or other proof of financial means).
- Other short-term (90/180 days) labourers (if permissible with Schengen visa and relevant national law)
- Letter from the employer and/or employment contract.
- Information about financial conditions of the visit.
- Members of train, refrigerator and locomotive crews 
- Written request from the competent Belarussian railway company or a competent railway company of Denmark stating the purpose, duration and frequency of trips.
- Proof of employment or copy of the work-book.
- Information about financial conditions of the visit.
- Participants in official twin city programs 
- Written request (letter) from the head of administration/mayor of the city where the twinning activity is to take place.
- Color copy of the official invitation from the head of administration/mayor of the sending city with full details of the participant.
- Air crews (if in need of a visa) 
- Letter from the operating body.

## Photo Specifications
Size of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the
                              head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above
                              format. 
Applicants may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
Average processing time for visa applications is 15 days, when all required documentation has been provided. This period does not include the time of delivery of documents from Minsk to London and back. Delivery may take up to 27 business days.
The case processing time is calculated from the day the Embassy register the application in the visa system. It is not calculated from the time you apply online or submit the application at a VFS centre. 
Please click here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service?anchor=09F4571341A14D82A430ACFF3113733A) for processing time information for Residence and Work Permits.

## Download Forms
Register your visa application online.

                              fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) ‘’ .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Submission of documents is carried out by preliminary registration through our website (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/blr/en/dnk/book-an-appointment). If you are planning to apply for a type C visa (Schengen visa), please select the “Short term - C visa” category when registering.
If you wish to visit Denmark for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Denmark.
Please visit Danish Immigration (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Short-stay-visa/Business-visits) for more details.
The Schengen calculator (http://ec.europa.eu/dgs/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/border-crossing/schengen_calculator_en.html) can be used for calculating the travel days remaining under a Schengen short-stay visa according to the 90-days/180-days rule.
The user's guide (https://ec.europa.eu/assets/home/visa-calculator/docs/short_stay_schengen_calculator_user_manual_en.pdf) contains information on the applicable rules, the use of the calculator and practical examples.

If you wish to visit Denmark for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Denmark.
Please visit Danish Immigration (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Short-stay-visa/Business-visits) for more details.
The Schengen calculator (http://ec.europa.eu/dgs/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/border-crossing/schengen_calculator_en.html) can be used for calculating the travel days remaining under a Schengen short-stay visa according to the 90-days/180-days rule.
The user's guide (https://ec.europa.eu/assets/home/visa-calculator/docs/short_stay_schengen_calculator_user_manual_en.pdf) contains information on the applicable rules, the use of the calculator and practical examples.

## Visa Fees

                                is levied per application over and above the visa fees

## Documents Required
This list is intended to help you compile the documents for your visa application. Please note that the Embassy of Denmark (https://storbritannien.um.dk/en) may ask for additional documents or further information if this is needed to assess the application.
Please note that all copies and printouts attached to the application must be in color.
Original documents must be presented along with the presented color copies. 
All documents should also have a translation into English.

Accredited Travel Agencies arrangement 
Documents to be submitted by the travel agency’s designated couriers 
Signed cover letter for an application form filled on https://applyvisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage). Signature must match the one in the passport. For minors: correctly signed application form by both parents. The application must be printed out in full in color (including the visa fee payment confirmation page).
- Foreign passport valid for at least three months after the intended date of (last) departure from the Schengen area, containing at least two blank pages and issued within the
                                  previous ten years.
                                  Additionally:
- If available other valid or/and expired foreign passports
- Two recent color photographs (3,5 x 4,5 cm) on the light background. Image has to bear a true likeness to the applicant. Photographs have to conform to the requirements of International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO). (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5Yzcxeg1mIivv7K7iVCq1v/78dca573e8e0a32b5254f31cdc701077/photograph-guidelines_5f0a8bf4945bbd14eb30b9c903750e4311cf71aa5d53dd822ee3ee23d9f576e0.pdf)
Due to the stringent requirements around the Photo required for your application, we recommend you utilize the photo services available inside the Visa Application Center. This
                                  will provide you assurance that your application is submitted with the photograph that meets the specifications and will be acceptable to the Embassy. You will have ready
                                  photographs in 45 seconds. 
                                  Fees: 
                                  The cost of the service is 360 RUB for 6 photographs.
                                  How to book: 
                                  This service can be booked at the visa application centre
                                  upon your arrival.

- Travel medical insurance covering the entire period of intended stay, valid for all Schengen States and covering any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for
                                medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be €30.000.
- Proof of funds with the history of 3 months, even if expenses are covered by the host.
- Transport documents (flight or other booking).
- Proof of accommodation.
- Color copy of the internal passport (issued from the age of 14): all pages
Please, pay attention, you will need to provide your internal passport in original as well
- In cases of minors:
- Consent of the parental authority or legal guardian should be required only if the minor travels alone or only with one parent. Exceptions should be made to this if the single
                                    parent with whom the minor is to travel holds the parental authority alone (i.e. in cases where the other parent has deceased or been deprived of custody, e.g. a death
                                    certificate of the other parent or a court degree vesting custody exclusively in the parent who is signing the application would be requested).

- *Official letter from the accredited Russian travel agency.

- *Hotel or other accommodation booking confirmation from Denmark, where family and given names of the applicant must be indicated, signed by the inviting part.
- *Return ticket or any other appropriate document indicating the envisaged travel plans; if not available, adequate written description of the planned journey.

                                  of real estate property in Russia, etc.).
Proof of employment:
                                  For employees – verification from the applicant’s employer with the company’s details, stamp and signature of an executive officer. It should include
                                  the following information:
                                  date of employment;
                                  position; information about salary.

                                  certificate of registration in the Tax Inspectorate, a copy of income statement for the accounting period stamped by Tax Inspectorate).
For students and scholars (over 14 years of age) – verification from university or school.
- Proof of financial means in the form of bank/credit card statement(s) covering at least three preceding months confirming the availability of minimum 70 EUROS per day or proof of
                                sponsorship.

- In cases of third-country nationals:

- If the application is not submitted personally by the applicant, the applicant must issue a notarized Power of Attorney to the person handing in the application. Also if the passport

Size of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the head

Danish Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above

                                  will provide you assurance that your application is submitted with the photograph that meets the specifications and will be acceptable to the Embassy. You will have ready
                                  photographs in 45 seconds.

                                  The cost of the service is 360 RUB for 6 photographs.

                                  This service can be booked at the visa application centre
                                  upon your arrival.
- Visa fee in line with the categories of the visa facilitation agreements with specific countries and the Visa Code.
- Travel medical insurance covering the entire period of intended stay, valid for all Schengen States and covering any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation
                                  for medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be €30.000.
The applicants are offered multiple options of insurance companies at the visa application center. It is available to purchase medical insurance here (https://www.alfastrah.ru/individuals/travel/vzr/?partner_id=6170637&country_group=manual).
- Proof of funds with the history of 3 months, even if expenses are covered by the host.
- Transport documents (flight or other booking).
- Proof of accommodation.
- Color copy of the internal passport (issued at the age of 14): all pages
Please, pay attention, you will need to provide your internal passport in original as well
- In cases of minors:
- Original and a Color copy of birth certificate.
- Consent of the parental authority or legal guardian should be required only if the minor travels alone or only with one parent. Exceptions should be made to this if the single
                                    parent with whom the minor is to travel holds the parental authority alone (i.e. in cases where the other parent has deceased or been deprived of custody, e.g. a death
                                    certificate of the other parent or a court degree vesting custody exclusively in the parent who is signing the application would be requested).
- Color copy of the valid Schengen visa of the parent(s) travelling with the minor for whom a visa is not applied at the same time with the parent(s).
- In cases of third-country nationals:
- Proof of legal residence in the Belarus, valid for at least three months after the planned return from the Schengen area or a document proving that the applicant has applied for a
                                renewal of proof of legal residence.
- If the application is not submitted personally by the applicant, the applicant must issue a notarized Power of Attorney to the person handing in the application. Also if the passport
                                  is collected by a third party, a notarized Power of Attorney is required.
For close relatives the document proving family relationship is sufficient.
                                  Accredited travel agencies and Red Carpet companies are exempted from this requirement
- Visa or other entry permit for the third country of destination, if applicable.
- Reserved tickets for onward journey.
It is strongly recommended not to buy the ticket until the visa is issued.
- Proof of employment with information about salary;); if not available (if the host is not financially responsible for all expenses regarding the trip), other proof of financial
                                  means and will to return (e.g. bank/credit card statement(s) covering at least three preceding months and confirming that the applicant has 50 EUROS per day, proof of sponsorship,
                                  or proof of real estate property in Russia).
Proof of employment:
For employees – verification from the applicant’s employer with the company’s details, stamp and signature of an executive officer. It should include the following information:
- date of employment;
- position;
- information about salary.
For self-employed people – Proof of economic activity: an original (and a copy) of the certificate of registration of an individual entrepreneur, an original (and a copy of the
                                  certificate of registration in the Tax Inspectorate, a copy of income statement for the accounting period stamped by Tax Inspectorate). 
For students and scholars (over 14 years of age) – verification from university or school.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.

Overview
Submission of documents is carried out by preliminary registration through our website (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/blr/en/dnk/book-an-appointment). If you are planning to apply for a type C visa (Schengen visa), please select the “Short term - C visa” category when registering.
If you wish to visit Denmark for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Denmark.
Please visit Danish Immigration (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Short-stay-visa/Business-visits) for more details.
The Schengen calculator (http://ec.europa.eu/dgs/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/border-crossing/schengen_calculator_en.html) can be used for calculating the travel days remaining under a Schengen short-stay visa according to the 90-days/180-days rule.
The user's guide (https://ec.europa.eu/assets/home/visa-calculator/docs/short_stay_schengen_calculator_user_manual_en.pdf) contains information on the applicable rules, the use of the calculator and practical examples.

For minors: correctly signed application form by both parents. The application must be printed out in full in color (including the visa fee payment confirmation page).
- Foreign passport valid for at least three months after the intended date of (last) departure from the Schengen area, containing at least two blank pages and issued within the
                                  previous ten years.
                                  Additionally:
- If available other valid or/and expired foreign passports
- Two recent color photographs (3,5 x 4,5 cm) on the light background. Image has to bear a true likeness to the applicant. Photographs have to conform to the requirements of International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO). (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5Yzcxeg1mIivv7K7iVCq1v/78dca573e8e0a32b5254f31cdc701077/photograph-guidelines_5f0a8bf4945bbd14eb30b9c903750e4311cf71aa5d53dd822ee3ee23d9f576e0.pdf)

- Consent of the parental authority or legal guardian should be required only if the minor travels alone or only with one parent. Exceptions should be made to this if the single
                                    parent with whom the minor is to travel holds the parental authority alone (i.e. in cases where the other parent has deceased or been deprived of custody, e.g. a death
                                    certificate of the other parent or a court degree vesting custody exclusively in the parent who is signing the application would be requested).

                                renewal of proof of legal residence.
- If the application is not submitted personally by the applicant, the applicant must issue a notarized Power of Attorney to the person handing in the application. Also if the passport
                                  is collected by a third party, a notarized Power of Attorney is required.
For close relatives the document proving family relationship is sufficient.
                                  Accredited travel agencies and Red Carpet companies are exempted from this requirement

It is strongly recommended not to buy the ticket until the visa is issued.
- Proof of employment with information about salary;); if not available (if the host is not financially responsible for all expenses regarding the trip), other proof of financial
                                  means and will to return (e.g. bank/credit card statement(s) covering at least three preceding months and confirming that the applicant has 50 EUROS per day, proof of sponsorship,
                                  or proof of real estate property in Russia).
Proof of employment:
For employees – verification from the applicant’s employer with the company’s details, stamp and signature of an executive officer. It should include the following information:
- date of employment;
- position;
- information about salary.
For self-employed people – Proof of economic activity: an original (and a copy) of the certificate of registration of an individual entrepreneur, an original (and a copy of the
                                  certificate of registration in the Tax Inspectorate, a copy of income statement for the accounting period stamped by Tax Inspectorate). 
For students and scholars (over 14 years of age) – verification from university or school.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.

## Photo Specifications
Size of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the head
                              should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above
                              format. 
Applicants may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
Average processing time for visa applications is 15 days, when all required documentation has been provided. This period does not include the time of delivery of documents from Minsk to London and back. Delivery may take up to 27 business days.
The case processing time is calculated from the day the Embassy register the application in the visa system. It is not calculated from the time you apply online or submit the application at a VFS centre. 
Please click here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service?anchor=09F4571341A14D82A430ACFF3113733A) for processing time information for Residence and Work Permits.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you need to submit biometrics for a residence permit, please select the “Residence Permit (biometrics)” category when registering (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/blr/en/dnk/book-an-appointment). Please ensure that you have paid all fees indicated in the Documents Required section and printed all payment confirmations in color before visiting the Visa Application Centre.
Many foreign nationals are free to live and work in Denmark. However, some are required to hold a residence and work permit - this is the case for Belarusian citizens. The specific requirements
                              in connection with living and working in Denmark depend, first and foremost, on a person’s nationality and qualifications.
It is your own responsibility to obtain a work permit if you are required to. If you work illegally in Denmark, you risk deportation, and you and your employer risk fine or imprisonment. 
Normally, professional or labour market considerations must warrant a residence and work permit, for example, if there is a lack of persons in Denmark who can carry out a specific type
                              of work. Read general information about salaried work. 
The rules also apply in the case of voluntary/unpaid work.
For more details Click Here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work)

## Processing Time

Overview
As a foreign national, you can be granted a residence permit in order to study in Denmark. There are three main categories of study which can warrant a residence permit: 
Higher educational programmes
Basic and youth study programmes
Folk high schools (folkehøjskoler) 
Read more about the various options and the application process here (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/studies/studies.htm)
