visa_types.documents_required:
This list is intended to help you compile the documents for your visa application. Please note that the Embassy of Denmark (https://storbritannien.um.dk/en) may ask for additional documents or further information if this is needed to assess the application.
Citizens of the Russian Federation must provide a biometric passport when applying for a type C visa. Non-biometric passports will not be accepted.
Citizens of Belarus can provide documents with both a biometric and non-biometric passport.
Please note that all copies attached to the application must be color copies.
Original documents must be presented along with the presented color copies. If you provide certified documents that are stapled together, you must additionally attach a color copy of them.
All documents should also have a translation into English. The translation may not be certified.
Note:
- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
GENERAL LIST OF REQUIRED DOCUMENTS
Close relatives visiting Danish citizens and citizens of other countries residing in Denmark:
- Signed cover letter for an application form 
filled on
https://applyvisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk)
. Signature must match the one in the passport. For minors: correctly signed application form by both parents. The application must be printed out in full in color (including the visa fee payment confirmation page).
- Signed current foreign passport (+ color copy of all pages) 
– issued within the last 10 years, have 2 blank pages and be valid for at least 3 months beyond your intended stay in the Schengen area. Color copies of passport pages with personal data must be provided in duplicate.
Additionally:
- If available, previous foreign passports – color copy of the all previous Schengen visas and stamps must be provided.
- For citizens of Belarus with a biometric passport: color copy of the ID card.
- For citizens of the Russian Federation: color copy of the internal passport (issued at the age of 14): all filled in pages.
- Two recent color photographs 
(3,5 x 4,5 cm) on the light background. Photographs have to conform to the requirements of
International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/denmark/Belarus/english/pdf/photograph-guidelines_5f0a8bf4945bbd14eb30b9c903750e4311cf71aa5d53dd822ee3ee23d9f576e0.pdf)
.
- Visa fee 
in line with the categories of the visa facilitation agreements with specific countries and the Visa Code.
- Travel medical insurance 
covering the entire period of intended stay, valid for all Schengen States and covering any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be €30.000.
- Proof of sufficient funds for the stay 
– original bank account statements showing the balance and your full name, covering the last 3 calendar months – approx. £40-60 per day.
- If sponsored by spouse: marriage certificate (color copy) + sponsorship letter + spouse’s bank statements.
- If sponsored by a parent: color copy of birth certificate + sponsorship letter + parent’s bank statements as per above.
- Invitation from host in Denmark 
–
online invitation (https://nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Short-stay-visa/Invitation-of-a-visa-applicant)
(VU2) ID number or signed and dated invitation incl. relationship with applicant and dates of visit. Host must provide a color copy of data page in passport and if applicable, proof of Danish residency. Family relationship must be documented.
- Proof of travel plan 
– e.g. reserved return ticket and reserved accommodation or sufficient means to cover such costs. The Embassy of Denmark in London recommends that you do not buy any tickets before your visa has been approved.
- Evidence of occupation 
– on letterhead, signed, and less than 1 month old.
- Employment: Letter from employer incl. salary and position of applicant and 3 months’ payslips. It should include the company’s details, stamp and signature of an executive officer
- Self-employed: proof of economic activity – an original (and a color copy) of the certificate of registration of an individual entrepreneur, an original (and a color copy) of the certificate of registration in the Tax Inspectorate, a copy of income statement for the accounting period stamped by Tax Inspectorate)
- Student: Letter of enrolment/confirmation of student status
- In cases of third-country nationals: 
- Proof of legal residence in the Belarus (and its color copy), valid for at least three months after the planned return from the Schengen area
- In cases of minors: 
- Original and color copy of birth certificate with both parents’ names 
. If one parent has sole custody or guardianship of the minor: Birth certificate mentioning one parent OR Death certificate of absent parent OR Court ruling
- Original and color copy of both parents' passport 
(or ID cards with signature) - If applicable, parents’ residence permit (and its color copy)
- Color copy of the valid Schengen visa 
of the parent(s) travelling with the minor for whom a visa is not applied at the same time with the parent(s)
- Consent of the parental authority or legal guardian 
(and its color copy) should be required only if the minor travels alone or only with one parent. Exceptions should be made to this if the single parent with whom the minor is to travel holds the parental authority alone (i.e. in cases where the other parent has deceased or been deprived of custody, e.g. a death certificate of the other parent or a court degree vesting custody exclusively in the parent who is signing the application would be requested).
Family members of EU (EEA and Swiss) citizens (not resident in their home country in line with Directive 2004/38/EC):
- Signed cover letter for an application form 
filled on
https://applyvisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk)
. Signature must match the one in the passport. For minors: correctly signed application form by both parents. The application must be printed out in full in color (including the visa fee payment confirmation page).
- Signed current foreign passport (+ color copy of all pages) 
– issued within the last 10 years, have 2 blank pages and be valid for at least 3 months beyond your intended stay in the Schengen area. Color copies of passport pages with personal data must be provided in duplicate.
	Additionally:
- If available, previous foreign passports – color copy of the all previous Schengen visas and stamps must be provided.
- For citizens of Belarus with a biometric passport: color copy of the ID card.
- For citizens of the Russian Federation: color copy of the internal passport (issued at the age of 14): all filled in pages.
- Two recent color photographs 
(3,5 x 4,5 cm) on the light background. Photographs have to conform to the requirements of
International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/denmark/Belarus/english/pdf/photograph-guidelines_5f0a8bf4945bbd14eb30b9c903750e4311cf71aa5d53dd822ee3ee23d9f576e0.pdf)
.
- Proof of EU/EEA/Swiss nationality 
– color copy of family member’s EU, EEA or Swiss passport + Original shown.
- Proof of family relationship with EU/EEA/Swiss family member 
– color copy of marriage certificate, birth certificate or other reliable proof of family relationship + Original shown.
- Proof of travelling together with or joining the EU/EEA/Swiss family member 
– Choice of any proof e.g. flight/hotel reservation, invitation, letter from family member etc. The Embassy of Denmark in London recommends that you do not buy any tickets before your visa has been approved.
- In cases of third-country nationals: 
- Proof of legal residence in the Belarus (and its color copy), valid for at least three months after the planned return from the Schengen area
- In cases of minors: 
- Original and color copy of birth certificate with both parents’ names 
. If one parent has sole custody or guardianship of the minor: Birth certificate mentioning one parent OR Death certificate of absent parent OR Court rulinga
- Original and color copy of both parents' passport 
(or ID cards with signature) - If applicable, parents’ residence permit
- Color copy of the valid Schengen visa 
of the parent(s) travelling with the minor for whom a visa is not applied at the same time with the parent(s)
- Consent of the parental authority or legal guardian 
(and its color copy) should be required only if the minor travels alone or only with one parent. Exceptions should be made to this if the single parent with whom the minor is to travel holds the parental authority alone (i.e. in cases where the other parent has deceased or been deprived of custody, e.g. a death certificate of the other parent or a court degree vesting custody exclusively in the parent who is signing the application would be requested).

visa_types.processing_time:
Average processing time for visa applications is 15 days, when all required documentation has been provided. This period does not include the time of delivery of documents from Minsk to London and back. Delivery may take up to 27 business days.
The case processing time is calculated from the day the Embassy register the application in the visa system. It is not calculated from the time you apply online or submit the application at a VFS centre.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Submission of documents is carried out by preliminary registration through our website (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/blr/en/dnk/book-an-appointment). If you are planning to apply for a type C visa (Schengen visa), please select the “Short term - C visa” category when registering.
If you wish to visit Denmark for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Denmark.
Please visit Danish Immigration (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Short-stay-visa/Business-visits) for more details.
The Schengen calculator (http://ec.europa.eu/dgs/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/border-crossing/schengen_calculator_en.html) can be used for calculating the travel days remaining under a Schengen short-stay visa according to the 90-days/180-days rule.
The user's guide (https://ec.europa.eu/assets/home/visa-calculator/docs/short_stay_schengen_calculator_user_manual_en.pdf) contains information on the applicable rules, the use of the calculator and practical examples.

## Photo Specifications
Size of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the
                              head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above
                              format. 
Applicants may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
Average processing time for visa applications is 15 days, when all required documentation has been provided. This period does not include the time of delivery of documents from Minsk to London and back. Delivery may take up to 27 business days.
The case processing time is calculated from the day the Embassy register the application in the visa system. It is not calculated from the time you apply online or submit the application at a VFS centre. 
Please click here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Diverse-US/Application-processing-times-in-the-Danish-Immigration-Service?anchor=09F4571341A14D82A430ACFF3113733A) for processing time information for Residence and Work Permits.

## Download Forms
Register your visa application online.

                              fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) ‘’ .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you have close relatives in Denmark, you can apply for a residence permit on the grounds of family reunification. 
Provided that certain conditions are met, family reunification can be granted to: 
Spouses, registered partners and cohabiting partners (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/familyreunification/spouses/spouses.htm)
Children (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/familyreunification/children.htm)
If you are granted a residence permit, it will be a temporary one. You can apply for an extension of your residence permit, and after a certain period of time, you can apply for a permanent
                              residence permit. You must still meet the conditions for your original residence permit.
Normally, a residence permit as a family reunified person gives you the right to work in Denmark.
Please note that special rules apply if the person residing in Denmark (the spouse/partner or the parent) is a student (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/studies/studies.htm).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
You can be granted a residence permit in order to function as an au pair with a host family in Denmark. 
                              It is your own responsibility to obtain a residence permit if you are required
                              to. 
The purpose of an AU pair stay
The term AU pair means 'on equal terms'. The idea of an AU pair stay is for a young person to stay with a host family with children under the age of 18 'on equal terms' with the other
                              members of the family. The purpose is for the AU pair to improve language and/or professional skills as well as broaden his/her cultural horizon by becoming more acquainted with Denmark.
                              In return, the AU pair participates in the host family's domestic chores (e.g. cleaning, washing clothes, cooking, babysitting) for a maximum of five hours a day, and no more than
                              six days a week.
If your chores exceed these time limits, or if you carry out work other than domestic chores, or carry out chores for someone other than your host family, then you are working illegally
                              in Denmark.
Duration
 You may be granted a residence permit for up to 24 months, but no longer than the duration of your AU pair contract. 
 If you have been granted a residence permit as an AU pair for less than 24 months, and you apply for an extension, you may continue as an AU pair while the Immigration Service is processing
                              your application, provided that you are with the same host family and that the conditions for your stay remain unchanged. 
 If you wish to begin with a new family, you may not do so before you have been granted a new residence permit.
Family members
 As an AU pair, you cannot normally bring your family to Denmark. 
 For most up-to-date and further information Click Here  (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/au_pairs/au_pairs.htm)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
You have submitted an application to SIRI, and you stay here legally while you wait for your reply (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/F%C3%A6lles/Procedural-stay/?anchor=DAAC1F1A1048457C9BC9DA62FD886DB9&callbackItem=5B21347563FB46CBBCEA03E4BF1DD83B&callbackAnchor=0780A5DEFD844BB681D2C35680E3BC02DAAC1F1A1048457C9BC9DA62FD886DB9).
Or
You already hold a residence permit, but you do not hold a residence card as proof of your permit.
If you, in these cases, need to leave and re-enter Denmark, you can apply for a re-entry permit.
The proof of your re-entry permit is a visa sticker put in your passport. 
N.B. If your trip takes place when you still hold a valid entry visa in your passport, you do not need a re-entry permit. Neither do you need a re-entry permit if you are exempt from the visa requirement (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Words-and-concepts/US/Visum/Countries-with-a-visa-requirement-and-visa-free-countries/?anchor=63F86BF9BAE84296B40028C26E06EF02&callbackItem=5B21347563FB46CBBCEA03E4BF1DD83B&callbackAnchor=0780A5DEFD844BB681D2C35680E3BC0263F86BF9BAE84296B40028C26E06EF02) and wait for a reply on your application for an extension of a residence permit.

visa_types.name: Residence and Work Permits
visa_types.overview:
Information for applicants who applies for residence permit in order to study in Denmark, application ST1, AU1 ( more than 90 days of stay). In order to be granted a residence permit you have to
                        prove that you can speak and understand and of have a working knowledge of either English, German, Danish, Swedish or Norwegian.
These applicants have to pass an interview at the Embassy of Denmark in Moscow in person the same day when the applicant submit his/her application at the Visa Centre VFS Global Services. You have
                        to make a preliminary appointment for handing in the documents and interviewing by Visa Centre’s telephone nr. 8 495 723 75 63 ( 9 am-16 am).
On these pages you will find information about residence and work permits to Denmark. For more details you can also visit Danish Immigration Service  (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/coming_to_dk.htm)website. 
Please be advised about changes regarding the Greencard Scheme and the Pay Limit Scheme with effect from the 10th of June 2016. For more information please see the following links:
- https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/News-Front-Page/2018/01/Lovforslag-om-ophaevelse-af-greencard-ordningen-er-vedtaget (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/News-Front-Page/2018/01/Lovforslag-om-ophaevelse-af-greencard-ordningen-er-vedtaget)
Information on Pay Limit Scheme: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work/Pay-limit-scheme (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work/Pay-limit-scheme)
DENMARK INTRODUCES BIOMETRIC RESIDENCE CARDS 20 MAY 2012 
From 20 May 2012 residence cards must contain digital photos, fingerprints and signatures. In this way the security against forgery and misuse of residence cards is strengthened. 
When you come to the visa application centre to deliver a residence/ work permit application, you will need to have your biometric data recorded.
Every applicant who is submitting a residence permit application must attend the visa application centre in person. 
Children of less than 6 years need to attend, but are not require to have fingerprints or signature recorded, only a photograph.
Exemption for fingerprints:
Children under the age of 6 
Applicants should bring loose leaf color original documents + color copies of all 
- supporting documents
- pages of the passport
in a standard format (A4 or legal). All copies must be in the same format. 
VFS Global constantly strives to offer top-of- the- line and revolutionary services to its clients around the world. Today, it is one of the few companies capable of integrating biometrics into the
                        application flow. 
Biometrics, a modern process used for biological identification of a person through fingerprints, iris, voice patterns, facial patterns, and hand measurements has proved to be an invaluable tool
                        to the Diplomatic Missions in ensuring absolute accuracy in identification. 
Triple Benefits 
VFS Global provides Diplomatic Missions with the unique benefits of interoperability, reliability and unmatched security. 
Interoperability 
- With existing systems used by Missions
- Several systems, one provider
- Capability of using common hardware by multiple Missions for enrolment
Reliability 
- Well trained and highly motivated enrolment staff to capture finger prints and facial images
- Only best quality images captured and converted as templates, as per the agreed quality benchmarks, which are inbuilt in the designed software
- An understanding of the requirements on quality standards ensures good quality templates at enrolment
Security 
- Spoof/liveliness /fake detection solutions
- Data transfer across borders

Information for applicants who applies for residence permit in order to study in Denmark, application ST1, AU1 ( more than 90 days of stay). In order to be granted a residence permit you have to
                        prove that you can speak and understand and of have a working knowledge of either English, German, Danish, Swedish or Norwegian.
These applicants have to pass an interview at the Embassy of Denmark in Moscow in person the same day when the applicant submit his/her application at the Visa Centre VFS Global Services. You have
                        to make a preliminary appointment for handing in the documents and interviewing by Visa Centre’s telephone nr. 8 495 723 75 63 ( 9 am-16 am).
On these pages you will find information about residence and work permits to Denmark. For more details you can also visit Danish Immigration Service  (http://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/coming_to_dk/coming_to_dk.htm)website. 
Please be advised about changes regarding the Greencard Scheme and the Pay Limit Scheme with effect from the 10th of June 2016. For more information please see the following links:
- https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/News-Front-Page/2018/01/Lovforslag-om-ophaevelse-af-greencard-ordningen-er-vedtaget (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/News-Front-Page/2018/01/Lovforslag-om-ophaevelse-af-greencard-ordningen-er-vedtaget)
Information on Pay Limit Scheme: https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work/Pay-limit-scheme (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply/Work/Pay-limit-scheme)
DENMARK INTRODUCES BIOMETRIC RESIDENCE CARDS 20 MAY 2012 
From 20 May 2012 residence cards must contain digital photos, fingerprints and signatures. In this way the security against forgery and misuse of residence cards is strengthened. 
When you come to the visa application centre to deliver a residence/ work permit application, you will need to have your biometric data recorded.
Every applicant who is submitting a residence permit application must attend the visa application centre in person. 
Children of less than 6 years need to attend, but are not require to have fingerprints or signature recorded, only a photograph.
Exemption for fingerprints:
Children under the age of 6 
Applicants should bring loose leaf color original documents + color copies of all 
- supporting documents
- pages of the passport
in a standard format (A4 or legal). All copies must be in the same format. 
VFS Global constantly strives to offer top-of- the- line and revolutionary services to its clients around the world. Today, it is one of the few companies capable of integrating biometrics into the
                        application flow. 
Biometrics, a modern process used for biological identification of a person through fingerprints, iris, voice patterns, facial patterns, and hand measurements has proved to be an invaluable tool
                        to the Diplomatic Missions in ensuring absolute accuracy in identification. 
Triple Benefits 
VFS Global provides Diplomatic Missions with the unique benefits of interoperability, reliability and unmatched security. 
Interoperability 
- With existing systems used by Missions
- Several systems, one provider
- Capability of using common hardware by multiple Missions for enrolment
Reliability 
- Well trained and highly motivated enrolment staff to capture finger prints and facial images
- Only best quality images captured and converted as templates, as per the agreed quality benchmarks, which are inbuilt in the designed software
- An understanding of the requirements on quality standards ensures good quality templates at enrolment
Security 
- Spoof/liveliness /fake detection solutions
- Data transfer across borders
